what_it_does: This file is a generated surface specification for view F4 in the activity area. It declares the view's identity, title, supported platforms, the event concepts it represents, its quality status, its home journey, the ui:// addresses it covers, and the components, lint command, and token source it must build to.
observations: id is F4, area is F, title is 'Activity / now + live event stream'
journeys and cross_journeys are empty arrays
addresses lists seven ui:// activity URIs including ui://activity, ui://activity/audit, ui://activity/audit-window, ui://activity/evt-now, ui://activity/heartbeat, and ui://activity/status
represents includes EVT-stream, EVT-now, and EVT-log
build_to names a single component 'activity', a design_lint command 'check.py --target canvas/app/src --fail-on', and a token pipeline from 'design/_system/tokens.json' to 'design-system.css'
the '_what' field says the directory is a journey spine (tree), journeys[]/cross_journeys[] carry the web (B3), and addresses were built by a Claude Code session and must never be invented
standouts: The surface is marked 'quality-passed' while journeys and cross_journeys are completely empty
The address list is present but not mapped into any journey or cross_journey structure
build_to tightly constrains both the lint gate and the single allowed look-source
conventions: Follows a ledger-style surface-spec schema with '_what', 'id', 'area', 'title', 'platforms', 'represents', 'status', 'home_journey', 'journeys', 'cross_journeys', 'addresses', and 'build_to'
Uses ui:// URI addresses for surface locations
Keeps generated provenance in '_what' rather than mixing it into the spec body
Separates routing concerns (journeys/cross_journeys) from address inventory
concerns: journeys and cross_journeys are empty, so the spec is declared quality-passed without actual journey wiring
The seven addresses are listed but their relationships to each other and to components are not explained
The design_lint value is a free-form shell command string, not a structured build target
The 'never invent an address' note implies the addresses came from a session, but no external provenance is recorded in the file itself
notes: A quality-passed but unrouted surface spec for the activity live-event view, listing seven ui:// addresses and strict build constraints without any journey wiring.
questions: Why is status 'quality-passed' when journeys and cross_journeys are empty?
How do the seven ui:// activity addresses map to the single 'activity' component?
What does the 'FORM gate' in the lint command enforce?
What is the B3 web that journeys/cross_journeys are meant to carry?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is a surface spec for the activity/now/live event stream view; the file matches that as an address and build-target declaration, but because journey arrays are empty it acts more like a scaffold awaiting routing than a fully wired surface.
apparent_themes: Journey-spine routing kept separate from surface inventory
Generated design artifacts with provenance notes
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to establish a canonical, lint-gated, single-source-of-truth surface for activity/now/live event streaming, while deferring the actual journey graph wiring to a later step.
novelty: The combination of a ui:// address registry, an explicit 'never invent an address' provenance warning, and build_to constraints that name both a FORM lint gate and a single token-derived CSS look-source.
contribution: A structured surface definition with an explicit address inventory, platform scope, represented event concepts, and build constraints that a fused system could retain as a precise build target for the activity view.
summary_for_embedding: F4.json is a generated, quality-passed surface specification for the desktop and mobile Activity now/live event stream view, listing seven ui:// activity addresses, leaving journey and cross-journey wiring empty, and targeting a single component, a form lint gate, and a token-derived CSS.
intention_for_embedding: To record the address set, platform scope, represented event concepts, and build constraints for the F4 activity surface so it can later be wired into journeys and built against a controlled design system.
